name: Travel Photography - A Practical UK Beginner's Guide

step.name: The night before: pack, charge, clear cards
step.text: Charge batteries, format cards in-camera, clean lens fronts, repack the bag with a weather cover accessible. Check weather and plan one landmark for first light.

step.name: First light: big landmarks, no crowds
step.text: Arrive 30 minutes before sunrise at your chosen landmark. First light gives warm directional colour and zero crowds — the biggest difference between snaps and a portfolio.

step.name: Flat midday light: markets, interiors, streets
step.text: Overhead sun kills landscapes but is fine for covered markets, museums, interiors, tight street scenes. Shoot details you missed at dawn. Eat at proper times.

step.name: Late afternoon: people, golden hour
step.text: The last 90 minutes before sunset is perfect for people, portraits, architecture with long shadows. Be where you want to shoot, not walking to it.

step.name: Blue hour: architecture with ambient light
step.text: Fifteen minutes after sunset, the sky goes deep blue and streetlights come on. Push ISO, brace against a wall. Window is 15-20 minutes. Eat after.

step.name: Back at the hotel: back up, charge, repeat
step.text: Back up cards to laptop, portable SSD, or cloud. Batteries on charge. Tomorrow's card cleared. Five minutes of discipline protects the week.
